raw_response:
{
  "claims_grounded_in_source": [
    "A person can properly part only with something they presently possess. (claude_sonnet_4_6, deepseek_v3, gpt_4o, grok_4)",
    "The past is not something a person currently possesses; it is gone. (deepseek_v3)",
    "The future is not something a person currently possesses; it is not yet. (deepseek_v3)",
    "Language about 'parting with' time is metaphorical, not literal. (deepseek_v3)",
    "The past and future lack the ontological status of objects that can be relinquished. (grok_4)",
    "Human relation to time is limited such that 'parting' applies solely to the immediate moment. (grok_4)"
  ],
  "claims_inferred_not_stated": [
    "True possession or ownership requires present, active holding — not mere memory or anticipation. (claude_sonnet_4_6)",
    "The past and future are ontologically distinct from the present in a way that matters morally and emotionally. (claude_sonnet_4_6)",
    "Loss, properly understood, can only occur with respect to things one currently possesses. (claude_sonnet_4_6)",
    "Much of human suffering over loss is based on a conceptual or linguistic misapplication of the word 'parting'. (claude_sonnet_4_6)",
    "There exists a human inclination or desire to 'part with' things, implying a wish for release from burdens, responsibilities, or attachments. (gemini_2_5_flash)",
    "Unlike certain other entities (e.g., possessions, current problems), the past and future are not amenable to being 'owned' or 'disposed of' in a literal sense. (gemini_2_5_flash)",
    "There is a 'proper' way of interacting with or letting go of things, and attempting to 'part with' the past or future falls outside this propriety due to their inherent nature. (gemini_2_5_flash)",
    "Our relationship with time is more abstract than concrete. (gpt_4o)"
  ],
  "hallucination_assessment": "The models largely maintain fidelity to the source's core meaning—that one cannot properly part with the past or future—but several introduce speculative extensions. For example, claude_sonnet_4_6 infers moral and emotional dimensions (suffering, misapplication) and gemini_2_5_flash adds a desire for release, both absent from the original non-propositional statement. While the source is tacit, models tend to infer propositional structure (e.g., ontological distinctions between past, present, and future) that is logically implied but not explicitly stated, which constitutes mild hallucination of content.",
  "inter_model_variance": "Models differ notably in specificity and scope: claude_sonnet_4_6 and gemini_2_5_flash infer psychological and moral implications, while deepseek_v3 and grok_4 stay closer to the literal meaning of possession and time. gpt_4o is vaguest, offering only abstract summaries."
}

claims_grounded_in_source: A person can properly part only with something they presently possess. (claude_sonnet_4_6, deepseek_v3, gpt_4o, grok_4)
The past is not something a person currently possesses; it is gone. (deepseek_v3)
The future is not something a person currently possesses; it is not yet. (deepseek_v3)
Language about 'parting with' time is metaphorical, not literal. (deepseek_v3)
The past and future lack the ontological status of objects that can be relinquished. (grok_4)
Human relation to time is limited such that 'parting' applies solely to the immediate moment. (grok_4)
claims_inferred_not_stated: True possession or ownership requires present, active holding — not mere memory or anticipation. (claude_sonnet_4_6)
The past and future are ontologically distinct from the present in a way that matters morally and emotionally. (claude_sonnet_4_6)
Loss, properly understood, can only occur with respect to things one currently possesses. (claude_sonnet_4_6)
Much of human suffering over loss is based on a conceptual or linguistic misapplication of the word 'parting'. (claude_sonnet_4_6)
There exists a human inclination or desire to 'part with' things, implying a wish for release from burdens, responsibilities, or attachments. (gemini_2_5_flash)
Unlike certain other entities (e.g., possessions, current problems), the past and future are not amenable to being 'owned' or 'disposed of' in a literal sense. (gemini_2_5_flash)
There is a 'proper' way of interacting with or letting go of things, and attempting to 'part with' the past or future falls outside this propriety due to their inherent nature. (gemini_2_5_flash)
Our relationship with time is more abstract than concrete. (gpt_4o)
parsed_json.hallucination_assessment: The models largely maintain fidelity to the source's core meaning—that one cannot properly part with the past or future—but several introduce speculative extensions. For example, claude_sonnet_4_6 infers moral and emotional dimensions (suffering, misapplication) and gemini_2_5_flash adds a desire for release, both absent from the original non-propositional statement. While the source is tacit, models tend to infer propositional structure (e.g., ontological distinctions between past, present, and future) that is logically implied but not explicitly stated, which constitutes mild hallucination of content.
parsed_json.inter_model_variance: Models differ notably in specificity and scope: claude_sonnet_4_6 and gemini_2_5_flash infer psychological and moral implications, while deepseek_v3 and grok_4 stay closer to the literal meaning of possession and time. gpt_4o is vaguest, offering only abstract summaries.
